seoTitle: Coombadjha vs Coombell — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Coombadjha and Coombell. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Coombadjha and Coombell.
heading: Coombadjha vs Coombell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Coombadjha, New South Wales and Coombell, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coombadjha has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Coombell (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Coombadjha or Coombell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Coombadjha has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Coombell scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Coombadjha scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Coombell scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Coombadjha or Coombell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coombadjha has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
